destinations.evidence: The reader defines the 10,000-service and 128-byte public-key bounds used to prevent unbounded allocation.
destinations.snippet:
pub const MAX_SERVICES: usize = 10_000;
pub const MAX_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN: usize = 128;

destinations.evidence: The all-executor read rejects excessive service counts before iterating and dynamic public-key reads reject lengths above 128 bytes before allocating/copying data slots.

equivalence_argument: The local reader uses MAX_SERVICES for the same 10,000 executor-count bound and MAX_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN for public-key length, returning explicit registry read errors instead of allocating unbounded memory.
notes: Mapped to the port TEE registry reader bounds.
rationale: The constants explicitly describe MAX_EXECUTORS and MAX_PUBLIC_KEY_LEN as DoS/OOM defenses, and the reader returns specific errors when contract storage exceeds those limits. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When reading all executors or public keys from ExecutorRegistry storage, the reader must reject executor counts above 10,000 and public keys longer than 128 bytes instead of allocating unbounded memory.
suggested_falsification_tests: Back a reader with storage slot 6 set to 10,001 and assert read_all_executors returns ExecutorCountExceeded, then store a dynamic public key length of 129 for an executor and assert public-key reads return PublicKeyLengthExceeded without reading arbitrary data slots.
summary: Registry reader storage reads enforce executor-count and public-key length bounds.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Set the service array length to 10,001 and a dynamic public key length to 129; assert read_all_executors/read_executor_public_keys return the configured bound errors.
title: Registry reader bounds executor and key reads
